payload.intent: Make the supervisor PRODUCE agentic worker receipts by spawning workers, instead of only grading caller-supplied ones. payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Make the supervisor PRODUCE agentic worker receipts by spawning workers, instead of only grading caller-supplied ones. The grade and enforcement path is already wired: run_dual_agent_workflow calls verify_dynamic_workflow_receipts / _evaluate_agentic_lead_policy at workflow_start and blocks before /lead runs. The supervisor-owned executor already exists in supervisor/agentic_workers.py: run_agentic_worker spawns a subprocess under .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/<worker>/, captures stdout/stderr/transcript/output, and sha256-hashes them; run_agentic_worker_fanout pools them. The missing link is that no production code produces the receipts. Connect producer to existing grader. Scope: wire run_agentic_worker_fanout into the execution path for agentic_lead_policy allowed|required; /lead plans a machine-readable read-only worker roster, supervisor validates and spawns, /lead synthesizes worker outputs into typed dual_agent_outcome, Codex validates with P13/P14 and independent review. Enforce read-only workers by rejecting writable roster specs, enforce per-agent timeout and budget caps, and call cleanup_orphaned_agentic_workers for timed-out/dead workers. Confirm .handoff/agentic-workers receipts derive runtime_native and required runs can satisfy required_evidence_grade=runtime_native while lead-self/non-supervisor-path and docs/dual-agent transcript/output refs do not over-credit runtime-native worker evidence. Also make solo_exception_for_artifact_only_gates gate-type-aware and add direct detached workflow job payload characterization coverage for all agentic policy fields. Non-goals: no eval harness, no native Claude TeamCreate fan-out, no default enablement, no gate-sequence weakening, no 4th reviewer registry, no raw MCP transport auto-reconnect.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Make the supervisor PRODUCE agentic worker receipts by spawning workers, instead of only grading caller-supplied ones. The grade and enforcement path is already wired: run_dual_agent_workflow calls verify_dynamic_workflow_receipts / _evaluate_agentic_lead_policy at workflow_start and blocks before /lead runs. The supervisor-owned executor already exists in supervisor/agentic_workers.py: run_agentic_worker spawns a subprocess under .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/<worker>/, captures stdout/stderr/transcript/output, and sha256-hashes them; run_agentic_worker_fanout pools them. The missing link is that no production code produces the receipts. Connect producer to existing grader. Scope: wire run_agentic_worker_fanout into the execution path for agentic_lead_policy allowed|required; /lead plans a machine-readable read-only worker roster, supervisor validates and spawns, /lead synthesizes worker outputs into typed dual_agent_outcome, Codex validates with P13/P14 and independent review. Enforce read-only workers by rejecting writable roster specs, enforce per-agent timeout and budget caps, and call cleanup_orphaned_agentic_workers for timed-out/dead workers. Confirm .handoff/agentic-workers receipts derive runtime_native and required runs can satisfy required_evidence_grade=runtime_native while lead-self/non-supervisor-path and docs/dual-agent transcript/output refs do not over-credit runtime-native worker evidence. Also make solo_exception_for_artifact_only_gates gate-type-aware and add direct detached workflow job payload characterization coverage for all agentic policy fields. Non-goals: no eval harness, no native Claude TeamCreate fan-out, no default enablement, no gate-sequence weakening, no 4th reviewer registry, no raw MCP transport auto-reconnect.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the supervisor PRODUCE agentic worker receipts by spawning workers, instead of only grading caller-supplied ones. The grade and enforcement path is already wired: run_dual_agent_workflow calls verify_dynamic_workflow_receipts / _evaluate_agentic_lead_policy at workflow_start and blocks before /lead runs. The supervisor-owned executor already exists in supervisor/agentic_workers.py: run_agentic_worker spawns a subprocess under .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/<worker>/, captures stdout/stderr/transcript/output, and sha256-hashes them; run_agentic_worker_fanout pools them. The missing link is that no production code produces the receipts. Connect producer to existing grader. Scope: wire run_agentic_worker_fanout into the execution path for agentic_lead_policy allowed|required; /lead plans a machine-readable read-only worker roster, supervisor validates and spawns, /lead synthesizes worker outputs into typed dual_agent_outcome, Codex validates with P13/P14 and independent review. Enforce read-only workers by rejecting writable roster specs, enforce per-agent timeout and budget caps, and call cleanup_orphaned_agentic_workers for timed-out/dead workers. Confirm .handoff/agentic-workers receipts derive runtime_native and required runs can satisfy required_evidence_grade=runtime_native while lead-self/non-supervisor-path and docs/dual-agent transcript/output refs do not over-credit runtime-native worker evidence. Also make solo_exception_for_artifact_only_gates gate-type-aware and add direct detached workflow job payload characterization coverage for all agentic policy fields. Non-goals: no eval harness, no native Claude TeamCreate fan-out, no default enablement, no gate-sequence weakening, no 4th reviewer registry, no raw MCP transport auto-reconnect.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Make the supervisor PRODUCE agentic worker receipts by spawning workers, instead of only grading caller-supplied ones. outcome.confidence_rationale: Public boundary, producer absence, grader wiring, solo-exception gap, and policy-field plumbing were each confirmed by reading source rather than trusting prose. Confidence is held below 0.9 because the 7 RED tests are unwritten and unrun (no executable evidence) and the allowed-policy path plus over-budget rejection are under-specified relative to stated scope.

assumptions_to_verify: That _agentic_subagent_summary currently derives evidence_grade from declared fields rather than path provenance (the RED premise for the downgrade test) - inferred from grade-min logic at lines 257-262 but the summary helper body was not read
That cleanup_orphaned_agentic_workers exposes injectable liveness/termination hooks suitable for the timeout test
contradictions_checked: Claim 'no production code produces receipts' vs run_agentic_worker_fanout existence - reconciled: function exists but has zero callers
Claim ISS-4 is regression not RED vs intent - reconciled: run_dual_agent_workflow already accepts all five policy kwargs, so characterization framing is correct
Claim solo-exception is not gate-aware - confirmed: _evaluate_agentic_lead_policy signature has no gate param
missing_evidence: Executable RED output for any of the 7 tests (none written yet)
A named test asserting allowed-policy non-blocking behavior
A named test asserting over-budget roster rejection before subprocess launch
Explicit assertion that failed/timed-out workers retain durable stdout/stderr/transcript refs
critical_review.strongest_objection: The plan's scope explicitly covers agentic_lead_policy=allowed|required, but every named Test Case exercises only the required policy, leaving the allowed path (fan-out should run but absence must not hard-block) without a public-boundary proof.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If _agentic_subagent_summary already derives runtime_native strictly from .handoff/agentic-workers provenance (making the downgrade test non-RED), or if an allowed-policy regression already exists elsewhere, the plan would need revision rather than straight acceptance.
